21.correct: The Indian Constitution provides for three types of emergency: National Emergency (Article 352), State Emergency / President's Rule (Article 356) and Financial Emergency (Article 360).

wrong.A: Two understates the number; there are three kinds of emergency.
wrong.C: Four is one more than the actual number of emergency provisions.
wrong.D: Five is incorrect; only three emergency types exist.

21.fact: Emergency provisions are contained in Part XVIII of the Constitution and were borrowed from the German (Weimar) idea.

22.correct: The Rajya Sabha is a permanent House that is never fully dissolved; one-third of its members retire every two years, and an equal number are freshly elected.

wrong.A: The President cannot dissolve the Rajya Sabha.
wrong.B: The Prime Minister has no power to dissolve the Rajya Sabha.
wrong.D: It is not dissolved every six years; rather, members serve six-year terms staggered so a third retire every two years.

22.fact: Because it is never dissolved, the Rajya Sabha is called a continuing or permanent chamber.

23.correct: The Nati is a fast, lively folk dance performed in a circle by both men and women, popular in the Himalayan region (Himachal Pradesh).

wrong.B: It is a group dance, not a solo performance.
wrong.C: It is not performed only by seated women.
wrong.D: It does not use swords and shields.

23.fact: A large Kullu Nati once set a Guinness World Record as the largest folk dance.

24.correct: Women performing the Bihu dance of Assam wear the Mekhela Chador, a traditional brightly coloured Assamese two-piece dress, with jewellery.

wrong.A: Heavily designed (zari) sarees are not the Bihu costume.
wrong.C: Plain cotton clothing is not the festive Bihu attire.
wrong.D: Salwar kameez is not the traditional Bihu dance costume.

24.fact: Bihu is the chief festival and folk dance of Assam, celebrating the agricultural cycle.

25.correct: The Ganga, together with the Brahmaputra, forms the world's largest delta - the Sundarbans delta - before emptying into the Bay of Bengal.

wrong.A: The Godavari forms a delta on the east coast but not the largest one.
wrong.C: The Krishna's delta is also on the east coast and far smaller.
wrong.D: The Yamuna is a tributary of the Ganga and does not directly form this delta.

25.fact: The Ganga-Brahmaputra delta covers parts of West Bengal (India) and Bangladesh.

26.correct: Die Brücke ('The Bridge'), a pioneering group of German Expressionist artists, was founded in 1905 in Dresden.

wrong.A: 1895 pre-dates the formation of the group.
wrong.B: 1925 is too late; the group had already dissolved by then.
wrong.D: 1915 is after Die Brücke's founding (and near its breakup).

26.fact: Ernst Ludwig Kirchner was a leading figure of Die Brücke.

27.correct: The Great Nicobar Islands lie closest to Indonesia; Indira Point, India's southernmost point, is very near the Indonesian island of Sumatra.

wrong.A: Myanmar lies to the north of the Andaman & Nicobar chain, not closest to Great Nicobar.
wrong.B: Sri Lanka is off the southern tip of mainland India, far from Great Nicobar.
wrong.D: Bangladesh lies to the north, near the Ganga delta.

27.fact: Indira Point on Great Nicobar is only about 150 km from Indonesia's coast.

28.correct: Kuyili (Velu Nachiyar's army commander) is regarded as India's first woman martyr / suicide attacker, who set herself ablaze to destroy a British arsenal.

wrong.B: Lakshmi Sehgal led the INA's Rani of Jhansi regiment much later, in the 1940s.
wrong.C: Matangini Hazra died in the 1942 Quit India Movement.
wrong.D: Jhalkari Bai was a soldier in Rani Lakshmibai's army during 1857.

28.fact: Velu Nachiyar of Sivaganga was one of the earliest queens to wage war against the British (late 1700s).

29.correct: If the star is like our Sun and the exoplanet orbits at an Earth-like distance, its surface temperature would be roughly the same as Earth's, since temperature depends mainly on the star's output and the planet's distance.

wrong.A: It would be significantly hotter only if it orbited much closer to the star.
wrong.C: It would be colder only if it were much farther from the star.
wrong.D: Distance does affect temperature, so 'no effect of distance' is wrong.

29.fact: The zone around a star where temperatures allow liquid water is called the 'habitable' or 'Goldilocks' zone.

30.correct: The Lakshmi Narayan Temple at Orchha is known for its unique blend of Hindu temple and Islamic (fort-like) architectural styles.

wrong.A: The Qutub Minar is an Indo-Islamic victory tower, not a temple blending the two styles.
wrong.C: This option does not name the temple known for the blend.
wrong.D: The Sun Temple at Konark is a purely Hindu (Kalinga-style) temple.

30.fact: Orchha, in Madhya Pradesh, was the capital of the Bundela Rajputs and is famous for its temples and palaces.

31.correct: The slow precession (wobble) of the Earth's axis is caused mainly by the gravitational pull of the Sun and the Moon on the Earth's equatorial bulge.

wrong.B: The speed of Earth's rotation does not cause the axial precession.
wrong.C: Variation in the orbit's shape (eccentricity) is a separate Milankovitch effect.
wrong.D: The solar wind is far too weak to cause precession.

31.fact: One full precession cycle of the Earth's axis takes about 26,000 years.

32.correct: Argentina won the first FIBA Basketball World Cup, held in 1950 (in Argentina).

wrong.A: The United States did not win the inaugural 1950 World Cup.
wrong.C: Brazil won later editions but not the first one.
wrong.D: Uruguay did not win the inaugural World Cup.

32.fact: The tournament was renamed the FIBA Basketball World Cup (from World Championship) in 2014.

33.correct: FIDE stands for Fédération Internationale des Échecs (International Chess Federation), the world governing body of chess.

wrong.A: 'Federation of International Digital Events' is a made-up expansion.
wrong.C: 'First International Dual Enterprise' is not what FIDE means.
wrong.D: 'Federation of Indian Domestic Events' is incorrect.

33.fact: FIDE was founded in Paris in 1924 and organises the World Chess Championship.

34.correct: Suberin is the waxy substance deposited in the cell walls of cork (and the root endodermis), making them impermeable to water and gases.

wrong.A: Cutin forms the protective cuticle on leaf surfaces, but the cell-wall waterproofing substance asked here is suberin.
wrong.B: Lignin provides rigidity to woody tissues but does not chiefly make cells gas/water-impermeable.
wrong.D: Cellulose is the main structural carbohydrate of cell walls but is permeable to water.

34.fact: Suberin in the Casparian strip of the root controls water and mineral uptake into the plant.

35.correct: A mitochondrion is bounded by two membranes - a smooth outer membrane and a folded inner membrane (whose folds are called cristae).

wrong.A: It has two membranes, not one.
wrong.C: Three is incorrect; mitochondria are double-membraned.
wrong.D: Four membranes is wrong.

35.fact: The inner-membrane folds (cristae) increase surface area for ATP (energy) production.

36.correct: By area, Madhya Pradesh has the largest forest cover among Indian states.

wrong.B: Assam has substantial forests but less total forest area than Madhya Pradesh.
wrong.C: Arunachal Pradesh has a very high percentage of forest cover but a smaller total area than MP.
wrong.D: Chhattisgarh has large forests but ranks below Madhya Pradesh in total forest area.

36.fact: As a percentage of geographical area, the north-eastern states (like Mizoram) lead, but in absolute area Madhya Pradesh is first.

37.correct: Per the government notification scrapping the 'no-detention policy' for Classes 5 and 8, students who fail the year-end exam are given extra coaching and a re-test within 2 months of the result.

wrong.B: 5 months is not the period specified.
wrong.D: 4 months is not the prescribed re-examination window.

37.fact: The change lets schools hold back students who fail even after the re-test, reversing automatic promotion.

38.correct: Under the Permanent Settlement (introduced by Lord Cornwallis in 1793), the zamindars' chief duty was to collect rent from the peasants and pay a fixed revenue to the Company.

wrong.A: Zamindars were intermediaries who collected rent, not cultivators of the land themselves.
wrong.C: Trading goods with the British was not the zamindar's defined duty.
wrong.D: Managing trade routes was not part of the Permanent Settlement obligations.

38.fact: The Permanent Settlement fixed the land revenue permanently, but harsh terms often ruined both zamindars and peasants.

39.correct: The British generally used the term 'vernacular' to refer to the local language or dialect of the people.

wrong.B: This option does not denote a local language.
wrong.C: 'Federal' refers to a system of government, not a local language.
wrong.D: A 'linguist' is a person who studies languages, not the term for the local tongue.

39.fact: The Vernacular Press Act of 1878 was used to muzzle Indian-language newspapers.

40.correct: Transparency International does NOT publish the Democracy Index (Statement I) - that is published by the Economist Intelligence Unit. Transparency International does publish the Corruption Perceptions Index (Statement II). So the correct choice is 'Only I'.

wrong.B: 'Only II' is wrong because TI does publish the Corruption Perceptions Index.
wrong.C: 'Both I and II' is wrong since TI does publish the Corruption Perceptions Index.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong because TI does not publish the Democracy Index.

40.fact: Transparency International is best known for its annual Corruption Perceptions Index (CPI).
